title: Thesis / Dissertation Outline
note: A chapter structure for an engineering/technology thesis, situated within AquaVision's phased software-to-hardware aquaculture platform, mapped to the repository files that supply each chapter. The engineering deep-dive remains the Phase 3 autonomous aerial subsystem — the OmniDrone concept — since that is what the design-and-feasibility thesis format (TRL 3) actually supports; the Phase 1 free app and Phase 2 premium tier are described as the business and data context the Phase 3 subsystem is built into, not as this thesis's own engineering contribution. Written for a DESIGN-AND-FEASIBILITY thesis, with a clear path to a results-bearing thesis once validation data exists on either layer.

the_honest_framing.what_kind_of_thesis_this_can_be_now: A design, specification, and feasibility study of AquaVision's Phase 3 autonomous aerial subsystem, situated within the phased platform it belongs to. It contributes a problem analysis spanning the whole platform, a Phase 3 system architecture, an identified engineering research gap, and a testable Phase 3 validation plan. It does NOT contribute empirical results for either the software funnel or the hardware, because neither has been built, launched, or flown.
the_honest_framing.what_it_becomes_after_validation: An empirical thesis once Phase 1-2 usage data and Phase 3 field data both exist — the app's activation and conversion behaviour is one empirical track (a separate, software-adoption study), and the drone's profile-vs-reference agreement, calibration-uncertainty curve, and stratification-precursor analysis are another (this thesis's own empirical track). They are publishable on different timelines and, most likely, in different papers.
the_honest_framing.the_discipline: Write in proposal/design tense now for the hardware, and in an equally cautious tense for any claim about the software platform's users or business performance, since neither exists yet. A committee will forgive an honest feasibility study; it will fail a study that claims hardware validation, software traction, or user behaviour it does not have.
the_honest_framing.the_tempting_error: Presenting the synthetic sample data as results, or presenting a hypothetical user-adoption or conversion curve as if it were observed. The illustrative_synthetic data exists ONLY to build the webpage; no app has shipped and no user has used it. Neither may appear in a results chapter. See sample-telemetry.json.

chapters.title: Introduction
contents: Background: the aquaculture inflection, and why almost none of the resulting industry is digitally managed at any price point

AquaVision's phased platform, briefly: a free software layer (Phase 1) and a premium software layer (Phase 2) that build an engaged user base and a longitudinal farm-data record, ahead of the autonomous aerial hardware layer (Phase 3) this thesis studies in depth
Problem statement
The reframe: monitoring as a labour-economics problem, and customer acquisition as a sequencing problem that a hardware-first company cannot solve cheaply
Research questions and objectives (scoped to the Phase 3 subsystem)
Scope and delimitation: the Phase 1/2 software layers are described as business and data context only, not as an engineering contribution of this thesis; what the Phase 3 system does NOT do
Significance of the study
Thesis structure
chapters.note: Use the problem taxonomy's coverage classification as the scope delimitation — it's already structured as inclusion/exclusion criteria. Keep the Phase 1/2 material to a few paragraphs; it motivates the Phase 3 design, it isn't a parallel subject of study here.

chapters.title: Review of Related Literature and Studies
contents: The five-move argument (aquaculture context → failure mechanism → monitoring gap → remote-sensing calibration problem → digital-twin observation famine)
Thematic synthesis across the eight study collections
The identified research gap
Counter-evidence and inconvenient findings
Theoretical framework

A brief situating note on why a software-first acquisition layer precedes the hardware in this platform's go-to-market, cited only to establish context for the Phase 3 data pipeline, not reviewed in the same depth as the UAV/water-quality literature
chapters.the_advice: Lead with the Laguna de Bay triad (DO + ammonia + H2S) — it's a published, mechanistic, Philippine finding that independently justifies the vertical-profiling design. Don't bury your best evidence. AND: verify every citation before submission — the bibliography currently has ZERO opened sources.
chapters.critical_warning: The bibliography is a scaffold, not a finished reference list. Every source must be retrieved and confirmed before this chapter is submitted. See references-bibliography.json#/CRITICAL_WARNING_READ_BEFORE_CITING.

chapters.title: Phase 3 System Design and Specification
contents: Where this subsystem sits in the platform: the hardware layer sold as an upgrade into farms already using Phases 1-2, not the company's first product
Concept of operations
Requirements baseline with traceability
Hardware architecture (airframe, winch, sensors, dispensing, power, dock)
Software architecture (pipeline, fusion, models, twin, telemetry, API)
The core architectural contribution: self-calibrating observation
Design trade-offs and the mass-budget finding
chapters.note: The mass-budget analysis (which does NOT close fully-configured) is a genuine engineering finding and belongs here honestly, not hidden. It demonstrates rigorous feasibility analysis — a strength, not a weakness.

chapters.title: Methodology
contents: Research design (mixed-methods, sequential)
The Phase 3 validation study design
The control-group problem and its ethical resolution
Data quality protocol
Analysis plan (Bland-Altman for agreement, calibration-uncertainty curves, mixed-effects for chronic loss)
Ethics
Validity threats
Falsification conditions
chapters.note: This chapter is unusually strong for a feasibility thesis because the methodology is pre-registered. The control-group ethical analysis and the explicit falsification conditions are genuine scholarly contributions. Foreground them. A companion methodology for validating the Phase 1/2 software (activation, retention, conversion) is out of scope for this thesis and belongs to a separate empirical study once the app exists.

chapters.title: Results
chapters.current_state: EMPTY. No results exist for the Phase 3 hardware, and no software-adoption data exists either. In the feasibility version of the thesis, this chapter is replaced by 'Feasibility Analysis' — the payload budget, the energy budget, and the risk analysis, which ARE completable now and constitute an analytical feasibility result.
chapters.the_hard_rule: Do NOT populate this with synthetic sample data, and do NOT populate it with a hypothetical app-usage or conversion curve. Ever.

chapters.title: Discussion
contents: Interpretation of the feasibility analysis
The system's contribution to the identified gap
Limitations (weather, benthic blind spot, affordability, unvalidated prediction)
A brief note on the business-model limitation this thesis does not resolve: Phase 3 customers only exist if Phase 1/2 acquisition and monetization work, which is itself unproven
The ethical tensions (over-stocking, antimicrobial use, equity, surveillance)
Comparison with alternatives (USV, buoy, satellite)
chapters.note: The limitations and ethical-tensions sections are where an honest thesis distinguishes itself. Include the over-stocking paradox and the affordability barrier as first-class discussion points, not buried caveats.

chapters.title: Conclusion and Recommendations
contents: Summary of contributions
Answers to the research questions (to the extent the feasibility study can answer them)
Recommendations for the next development phase, including sequencing Phase 3 field validation against Phase 2 customer emergence rather than a fixed calendar date
Future work (the Phase 3 validation programme, and, separately, a software-adoption study once the app has real users)

front_and_back_matter.abstract: See elevator-pitches.json#/the_thesis_abstract_version — written in honest proposal tense.
front_and_back_matter.references: references-bibliography.json — with the standing warning that every entry must be verified before submission.

what_this_thesis_genuinely_contributes: A structured problem analysis grounding a documented, recurring, mechanistically-understood failure mode in published evidence.
A reframing of the monitoring problem as labour-economics rather than instrumentation, and of customer acquisition as a sequencing problem a hardware-first company cannot solve cheaply.

A system architecture whose central novelty is self-generated calibration ground truth, addressing a gap the UAV water-quality literature repeatedly identifies — specified as the Phase 3 layer of a phased software-to-hardware platform.
A feasibility analysis (mass, energy, risk) that honestly reports where the Phase 3 concept does and does not close.
A pre-registered validation methodology including an ethical resolution to the control-group problem and explicit falsification conditions.
the_contribution_statement.what_it_does_not_claim: Empirical validation of any performance or predictive capability for the hardware, and no claim whatsoever about software user adoption, retention, or conversion — none of that data exists. Both are future work, and the thesis says so plainly.
the_contribution_statement.why_this_is_a_legitimate_thesis: A design-and-feasibility study with a rigorous problem analysis, an identified gap, a specified system, and a pre-registered method is a complete and defensible scholarly contribution at this stage. Situating that Phase 3 system honestly within the company's phased go-to-market strengthens the significance argument without expanding the thesis's empirical claims beyond what it has actually built. It becomes a stronger, results-bearing thesis after the validation season — but it does not need to overclaim, on either layer, to be worth defending now.
